mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production directly expands the supply side of the economy, boosting corporate earnings and investor sentiment, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO monthly change vs 20d forward return is below 0.02 over any three-year rolling window or t-test p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above the near-zero floor after an extended period, it signals the start of monetary tightening that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% after being at or below 0.25% for at least 6 months
failure_condition: If SPX produces positive average 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or if the t-test comparing triggered vs non-triggered periods is not significant at 10%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising retail sales and falling unemployment indicate robust consumer demand driven by income growth, which boosts corporate revenues and stock prices. The joint behavior amplifies the signal beyond each series individually.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (RSAFS growth × UNRATE decline) in a linear regression of 20-day returns is not statistically significant at p < 0.10, or if the composite signal's Rank IC < 0.03 over three-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During the early recovery phase when unemployment is still elevated but falling, rising housing starts signal durable demand and building confidence, leading to above-average equity returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 6% and UNRATE declining over the previous 3 months
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return in triggered periods is not significantly higher than in non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.10), or if HOUST change alone has Rank IC < 0.02 in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in consumer price inflation above 0.3% raises fears of Federal Reserve tightening, which increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: If SPX produces positive average 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or if the average return difference between triggered and non-triggered periods fails a one-sided t-test at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a strengthening labor market that supports aggregate consumption and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of PAYEMS monthly change vs 60-day forward return is not significantly greater than zero at p < 0.10 over any five-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero and industrial production posts strong monthly growth, the combination of ultra-loose monetary policy and expanding supply creates a powerful tailwind for equities as earnings rise without immediate tightening risk.
failure_condition: If the interaction coefficient in a regression of 20-day returns on FEDFUNDS level and INDPRO growth is not positive and significant at p < 0.10, or if the composite triggered signal's Rank IC < 0.03 over any two-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment (CPI year-over-year below 2%), rising real retail sales signal genuine demand expansion without eroding purchasing power, which is especially favorable for equity returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2% and RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return following triggered months is not significantly greater than the sample mean (t-test p > 0.10), or if RSAFS growth alone (without inflation filter) does not exhibit higher predictive IC in low-inflation periods compared to high-inflation periods.
